answers.value:
Project context: Northwest Property Management, LLC recently acquired the former Hawthorne Plaza Apartments (constructed circa 1968–1972) for planned interior renovation and partial façade replacement. Prior to initiating any demolition or disturbance of suspect materials, an asbestos survey is being performed in accordance with Oregon DEQ and OSHA requirements. 

Scope of sampling: On 2026-01-18, Cascade Environmental Consulting, Inc. (field lead: Emily Carson) collected a targeted set of bulk samples of suspect asbestos-containing materials (ACMs) from representative locations throughout the property. The primary focus was on interior finishes (vinyl floor tile, associated mastics, drywall joint compound, and ceiling textures) and limited exterior materials (window glazing and roofing patch) that are likely to be disturbed during the planned renovation. The building was fully vacant at the time of sampling. Power and lighting were available on all floors; access to all sampled areas was unobstructed.

Sampling methodology: Bulk samples were collected following EPA and OSHA recommended practices. For each homogeneous area, at least 2–3 discrete grab samples were collected where accessible, focusing on areas of visible deterioration, previous patching, or varied installation dates. Tools were cleaned between samples to minimize cross-contamination. Each sample was placed in a new, clean, sealable container (4-mil zip-top bag), labeled on both the bag and the COC with a unique sample ID, date, and collector initials. Nitrile gloves were changed between materials and whenever they became visibly dusty.

Chain-of-custody handling: Immediately after collection, all samples were boxed in a rigid cardboard shipping carton with packing material to prevent breakage. The box was sealed with tamper-evident tape. The box remained in the custody of the field lead (E. Carson) from collection through direct delivery to ACM Engineering & Environmental Services, Inc. laboratory reception on 2026-01-18. No third-party courier was used. The field team maintained a field logbook documenting sample IDs, locations, and any anomalies (e.g., prior repairs).

Special instructions for analysis: 
1) Please perform polarized light microscopy (PLM) bulk asbestos analysis on all submitted samples in accordance with EPA Method 600/R-93/116 or equivalent. Where applicable (e.g., multi-layer flooring systems, joint compound over drywall, textured ceiling coatings), please separate and analyze each distinct layer individually and report both layer-specific and composite results. 
2) For any samples that visually appear to contain low asbestos content near the regulatory threshold, or where PLM results are reported as <1% asbestos, please note this clearly in the report and flag them for potential follow-up point-count analysis. At this time, routine point-count is not requested for all samples, but we may request it on specific samples once initial PLM results are reviewed. 
3) Please identify asbestos type(s) (e.g., chrysotile, amosite, crocidolite, etc.) and provide estimated percent content by visual estimation for each asbestos-positive layer. For non-detected samples, please indicate "none detected" with method detection limitations noted in your standard reporting format.

Project-specific concerns: 
- The 9"x9" vinyl floor tile and associated black mastic in the basement storage corridor (samples HP-01 and HP-02) are of particular concern because they are extensively cracked and partially delaminated from the concrete substrate. These areas are likely to be removed by mechanical scraping. Please ensure careful layer separation between tile and mastic, and clearly distinguish asbestos content in each.
- The sprayed-on acoustic ceiling texture in the third-floor corridor (samples HP-05 and HP-06) appears to be original to the building and shows localized water staining from past roof leaks. We are particularly concerned about the potential for friable ACM in these areas. Please note friability observations in your internal records if you make any during sample preparation.
- The exterior window glazing compound (sample HP-09) and roofing patch material (sample HP-10) were collected from weathered locations that may have undergone prior spot repairs. If multiple visually distinct layers are present (e.g., original glazing vs. newer caulk overlay, or original roof patch vs. newer sealant), please analyze them separately where practical.

Reporting needs: 
- Please provide a clear, tabulated report listing each sample ID exactly as shown on this chain-of-custody, including sample description, location, homogeneous area designation (if used), analytical method, asbestos type(s), and percent content. 
- Electronic report (PDF) should be emailed to: mhanley@nwpropertymgmt.com and copied to ecarson@cascade-env.com. If your system allows, please also provide an Excel summary of results to facilitate integration into our survey report tables.
- If any samples are found to contain >1% asbestos, please highlight these in the report summary or provide a separate list of "Regulated ACM" for easy reference by the client and abatement contractor.

Turnaround expectations: Standard 2–3 business day turnaround is acceptable for this project, as indicated below. However, if you anticipate any delays due to workload or instrument downtime, please notify both Michael Hanley (client) and Emily Carson (consultant) as soon as possible so that renovation scheduling can be adjusted.

Health and safety notes: All sampling was completed with appropriate PPE (half-face respirators with P100 filters, disposable coveralls, safety glasses, and nitrile gloves). No airborne sampling was conducted during this site visit; this submission is limited strictly to bulk material analysis. No known chemical contamination (e.g., heavy oils, solvents) was observed in the collected materials.

If you require any clarification regarding sample locations, building history, or planned renovation scope, please contact Emily Carson at (503) 555-7462 or via email at ecarson@cascade-env.com. She maintains the full field notes, floor plans with sample locations, and photographic documentation for this project.
